Front: GCP: the unified ML platform and the ladder of ML options (least -> most custom).
Back: Vertex AI is the unified ML platform (umbrella; analog of AWS SageMaker). Options ladder: (1) Pre-trained APIs (call Google's model - Vision, Speech-to-Text, Translation), (2) BigQuery ML (train in SQL on BigQuery data), (3) AutoML (low/no-code custom models on your data), (4) Custom training (your own code, full control). Pick the lowest tier that solves it. BigQuery ML is a TIER, not the umbrella.

Front: GCP: pre-trained API vs AutoML - when to use which?
Back: Pre-trained API = Google's own model for a generic, solved task (speech-to-text, translation, vision) - just call it, no data/training. AutoML = your CUSTOM model trained on YOUR labeled data, for a task no pre-built model fits. Example: transcribing support calls = pre-trained Speech-to-Text API; classifying your own product defects = AutoML.

Front: AI hardware ladder: CPU vs GPU vs TPU (and AWS equivalents).
Back: CPU = general-purpose, few powerful cores, versatile/sequential. GPU = thousands of cores, massively parallel (matrix ops). TPU = Google's custom ASIC purpose-built for tensor/matrix operations, most specialized. Each trades flexibility for ML performance. AWS equivalents: Trainium (training) + Inferentia (inference) ~ TPU; SageMaker ~ Vertex AI.
